does: This paper describes a structured red-teaming competition designed to identify universal jailbreaks and adversarial prompts that could bypass safety measures in GPT-5.5 when applied to biosecurity-relevant tasks. The challenge provides participants with direct access to the model and financial incentives to systematically discover failure modes related to biological risks, collecting real-world adversarial examples and attack strategies. The work establishes a methodology for crowdsourced vulnerability discovery in large language models, documenting which attack techniques prove most effective at circumventing guardrails and enabling concerning outputs about dangerous biology.
connects: This builds on growing recognition within AI safety that red-teaming and adversarial testing are essential for identifying gaps in model safety training before deployment. It extends prior work on jailbreaking techniques, prompt injection vulnerabilities, and the challenge of securing models against adversarial inputs, while specifically focusing on biosecurity as a high-stakes domain. The bounty structure draws from successful security research traditions in software and cybersecurity, adapting responsible disclosure practices and bug-bounty frameworks to the emerging domain of AI safety, and complements internal safety testing with external expertise to uncover harder-to-find vulnerabilities.
enables: This work generates a dataset of novel jailbreak techniques and failure modes that OpenAI and the research community can use to improve safety training and red-teaming procedures for future models. The findings directly inform which attack vectors remain problematic and where defenses are weakest, enabling more targeted mitigation strategies. More broadly, it establishes a replicable model for biosecurity-focused safety research on frontier AI systems, potentially inspiring similar bounty programs for other high-risk capabilities and demonstrating how coordinated external security research can strengthen AI safety practices before deployment at scale.
